summary: Jeff Atwood enthusiastically recommends the AMD Athlon 64 X2 dual-core processor for developers, noting its wide availability and superior compilation speeds. He highlights the newly released budget-friendly X2 3800+ at under $400 and suggests the retail package is worth the premium for its excellent stock heatsink. The post frames the purchase as practical preparation for Visual Studio 2005's increased system requirements.
key_points: The Athlon 64 X2 offers unmatched code compilation performance for developers
AMD's new X2 3800+ brings dual-core within reach at under $400
The retail package includes an unusually high-quality stock heatsink with copper inlay and heatpipes
VS.NET 2005's higher system requirements justify the hardware upgrade
money_quotes: As a developer, you will not find a CPU that compiles code faster.
In fact, it's not even close.
You'll certainly need something to counteract VS.NET 2005's higher system requirements.
And you get an AMD X2 sticker, too. A STICKER!
key_insight: Dual-core processors have become practical and affordable enough that developers should upgrade now for dramatically faster compilation times.
